metadata.title: Why a helpful Claude invents an answer — and permission to say "I don't know" stops it
metadata.topic: Helpfulness pressure as a hallucination source
metadata.purpose: Two low-cost gates cut hallucination — give Claude an explicit out ("say you don't know if unsure") and force evidence-before-answer (pull verbatim quotes first), so an unsupported claim has nowhere to hide.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.visual_object: A document with a highlighted distractor passage; an "extract quotes" pass returns an empty quote box, collapsing the false answer
metadata.hook: Handed a long document with almost-relevant distractor text, Claude tries so hard to be helpful that it confidently answers a question the document never actually addresses.

beats.narration_text: Asked for Matterport's subscriber count "as of May 31, 2020," Claude latches onto nearby distractor figures and states a wrong number. Told to first extract the exact quotes that answer the question, it finds none match — and correctly reports the document doesn't say.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: More context should mean more accuracy; the extra document made Claude more confidently wrong; why does "quote the evidence first" reverse it?
Handed a long document with almost-relevant distractor text, Claude tries so hard to be helpful that it confidently answers a question the document never actually addresses.

Two low-cost gates cut hallucination — give Claude an explicit out ("say you don't know if unsure") and force evidence-before-answer (pull verbatim quotes first), so an unsupported claim has nowhere to hide.
beats.visual: A document with a highlighted distractor passage; an "extract quotes" pass returns an empty quote box, collapsing the false answer
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Question about a figure not in the doc. No guard → "≈2 million subscribers" (invented). Quote-first → "no quote answers this."

Two low-cost gates cut hallucination — give Claude an explicit out ("say you don't know if unsure") and force evidence-before-answer (pull verbatim quotes first), so an unsupported claim has nowhere to hide.
